entries.decision: F2 (load-bearing): structured output via an opt-in global --json flag; human text stays the default and byte-for-byte unchanged
entries.rationale: Opt-in is the only back-compat-safe option: existing tests and the operator read the current human stdout. With --json every verb emits one structured JSON result; without it, nothing changes. One flag, uniform across verbs.

entries.decision: F1: add a 'lume verbs' discovery verb emitting the verb catalog (name, summary, arg outline); joins the entities/schema/get discovery family
entries.rationale: tools/list analogue, consistent with the existing read-only discovery verbs; lets an agent enumerate commands without parsing the usage prose. stdlib-only.

entries.decision: F3: under --json, errors emit a JSON object {error:{code,message}} on stderr; exit codes unchanged (2 usage, 1 domain/gate, 0 ok); code is a small stable string enum
entries.rationale: Keeps the existing two-tier exit-code strength as the primary signal and adds a parseable body + stable code for agents. Human stderr string preserved when --json absent.

entries.decision: F4 (per-verb input schema, principle P2): DEFER to an optional later iteration
entries.rationale: Valuable but larger and depends on the --json result shape landing first; adopting it now risks freezing the arg grammar prematurely. Revisit after the core ships.

entries.decision: F5 (grammar standardisation, principle P4): NARROW - document the grammar; no breaking changes this workstream
entries.rationale: Caller/test safety outweighs the marginal tidiness; the rough edges (positional shapes, get plan alias) are not blocking agent use once discovery + --json exist.

entries.decision: F6 v1 cut: adopt P1 (verb discovery) + P5 (structured --json output) + P6 (structured errors); defer P2 (input schema) and P4 (grammar)
entries.rationale: These three are the high-value core that lets an agent drive lume without scraping prose; P2/P4 are deferrable polish. Matches the discovery recommendation.
